todaysEvents.summary: All 12 P&I clubs (full International Group, ~90% of ocean-going tonnage) confirmed exit from Gulf war risk cover effective midnight London March 5 — creating a complete commercial insurance blackout. VLCC spot war risk premiums at 3.5–4.5% hull value/voyage. Trump administration announces USN vessel escort program for neutral-flagged tankers and sovereign war risk guarantee; operational scope being finalised with CENTCOM — physical transits remain halted for most commercial operators pending escort parameters (Mar 4)

OPEC+ emergency response disappoints: 8-member subset added only +206,000 bpd for April (Saudi +62k, Russia +62k, Iraq +26k, UAE +18k) vs ~3.5M bpd declared spare capacity — signaling strategic restraint. Key paradox: GCC spare capacity cannot reach market while Hormuz blocked. Brent consolidating at $83–85/bbl; BloombergNEF maintains $91 target under sustained disruption; J.P. Morgan upper-end at $130 (Mar 4)

Iran's Assembly of Experts convened in Qom to vote for successor Supreme Leader; no frontrunner after Day 1 of deliberations under Israeli airstrike threat. IRGC retaining effective command in interim. UAE stock exchanges (DFM, ADX, Nasdaq Dubai) reopened March 4 after 2-day suspension. Indonesia energy minister announces shift of Middle East crude import orders to US suppliers amid Hormuz closure; 1,400+ Philippine OFWs formally requesting repatriation (Mar 4)
todaysEvents.delta: Iran succession / UAE reopens / OFW crisis

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Hormuz disruption lasts 2–4 weeks; Iran successor government enters ceasefire via diplomatic back-channel. Trump's naval escort program partially restores tanker flow within 10–14 days. Brent stabilises at $82–90/bbl with sustained risk premium. Saudi/UAE spare capacity (3-week ramp) partially offsets supply deficit. ASEAN LNG buyers scramble for spot cargoes; JKM peaks near $20/MMBtu. SCS tensions remain elevated but secondary to Iran shock. P&I clubs restore cover upon ceasefire confirmation. Goldman Q4 2026 normalisation forecast at $60/bbl contingent on swift resolution.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Hormuz closure extends 1–3 months; Iran successor IRGC-led government escalates proxy network. P&I blackout (effective Mar 5) proves durable. Trump escort provides minimal relief — capacity to escort 2–3 tankers/day vs pre-crisis 20M bpd flow. Brent surges toward $100–110/bbl (BloombergNEF $91 base case; J.P. Morgan upper-end $130). JKM LNG spikes to $25+/MMBtu — worst gas market shock since Russia-Ukraine 2022. ASEAN current account deficits widen sharply; Bank Indonesia and BSP forced into emergency interventions. Indonesia subsidy blowout accelerates.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Full Hormuz closure sustained beyond 3 months simultaneously with SCS kinetic escalation. Oil at $150–200/bbl (J.P. Morgan upper bound). LNG effectively unavailable for spot buyers; Qatar's 77 mtpa offline. Saudi/UAE spare capacity cannot compensate — Hormuz transit required to reach market. Recession probability >70% for Thailand and Philippines. ASEAN EM bonds face mass outflows; 1970s-style energy shock across the region. P&I cover does not return for 6+ months; global trade finance seizes.
